sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 45-year-old male, presented with an ankle injury sustained while playing basketball with his son.
He described landing awkwardly after being fouled by his son, resulting in a twisted and rolled ankle.
He reported pain on the outside of the ankle, swelling, and bruising.
He has been experiencing difficulty walking and feels the ankle is not stable.
The patient has been working out regularly and is concerned about disrupting his routine.
Past medical history: [NOT MENTIONED]
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient's ankle was found to have exquisite tenderness laterally, tenderness over the medial deltoid region, and swelling on the lateral side.
The skin is intact with brisk capillary refill.
The ankle does not exhibit malalignment.
Test Results: The patient had an X-ray taken, which revealed a displaced lateral malleolus fracture at the Weber C level, with no evidence of medial or posterior malleolar fractures.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a displaced lateral malleolar fracture.
Problem list: Lateral malleolar fracture; suspected instability; [NOT MENTIONED]
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam to reduce swelling.
I advised the patient to ice the ankle and keep it elevated.
I also recommended that the patient use crutches to avoid further injury.
I suggested that the patient continue coaching in April, but advised him to avoid putting weight on the ankle for approximately three months.
I will follow up with the patient in the future to assess his progress.
Follow-up: [NOT MENTIONED]
